sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano are in decent form, remaining unbeaten in their last 4 matches with 2 wins and 2 draws, showcasing resilience and late-game scoring ability. Deportivo Alavés have had mixed results, managing 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses in their last 5, with a strong tendency for low-scoring games as seen in 6 of their last 8 matches being under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, Rayo Vallecano have the edge with 2 wins, including a 1-0 victory at home last season and a 2-0 away win earlier this year. Matches between these sides tend to be low-scoring, with all 7 of their recent head-to-heads featuring under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano (12th, 11 points) boast a strong defensive record, conceding just 10 goals (3rd fewest in the league), and excel in ball possession (5th, 53.2%) and corners per game (4th, 6.3). Deportivo Alavés (11th, 12 points) also have a solid defense (8 goals conceded), but struggle offensively, scoring only 9 goals, with late goals (4 in the 76-90th minute) being their forte.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano's ability to create big chances (7th in the league) and tendency to score first (4/5 recent matches) gives them a slight edge at home. Alavés' games often see high card counts (7/8 over 4.5 cards), and their away form has been inconsistent, which could play into Rayo’s hands.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Rayo Vallecano's home advantage, unbeaten streak, and head-to-head dominance, they are slight favorites to win with a 40% probability as per Sofascore’s model. Expect a tight, low-scoring match with over 4.5 cards and a high corner count, aligning with both teams' recent trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Rayo Vallecano and Deportivo Alavés at Estadio de Vallecas ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Rayo Vallecano dominated possession and showed more intent in creating opportunities, particularly through their higher number of shots on target. However, both teams had big chances but failed to convert, indicating a lack of clinical finishing. The game has been competitive, with both sides showing moments of promise but also defensive resilience.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano have looked the more likely to score in the first half, creating a notable big chance and registering several shots on target. Their dominance in ball possession allowed them to control the tempo for significant periods. Players like Fran Pérez and Jorge de Frutos have been active in the attack, contributing to dangerous moves. However, missing a key opportunity suggests they need to be sharper in front of goal. Defensively, they’ve held up well, with players like Florian Lejeune making crucial interventions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had their moments in the first half, matching Rayo Vallecano in creating a big chance, though they couldn’t find the target with their efforts. Their slightly lower possession share reflects a more cautious approach, focusing on counter-attacks and set-pieces, as evidenced by a decent number of corner kicks. Defensively, they’ve been solid, with players like Jon Pacheco stepping up to clear threats. However, their lack of shots on target highlights a struggle to test the opposition goalkeeper.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Rayo Vallecano started with intent, pushing forward early on, but Alavés quickly responded with a spell of pressure. The momentum shifted back to the home side around the middle of the half with sustained attacking phases. Towards the latter stages of the half, Rayo Vallecano mounted significant pressure with consistent forward surges, while Alavés struggled to regain control, managing only brief moments of threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key stats and discipline
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano edged the statistical battle in the first half with a higher share of possession and more entries into the final third. Their expected goals metric suggests they’ve had better quality chances compared to Alavés. On the discipline front, both teams picked up a yellow card each, indicating a physical but controlled encounter so far. Neither side has been overly dominant in duels, with Alavés slightly ahead in overall challenges won.
